tools.name: File Operations
tools.description: Reading, writing, and manipulating files in Python
examples: with open('data.txt', 'r') as f: content = f.read()
with open('output.txt', 'w') as f: f.write('Hello World')
tools.notes: Always use context managers (with statement) for file operations to ensure proper cleanup

tools.name: Regular Expressions
tools.description: Pattern matching and text manipulation using regex
tools.notes: Use raw strings (r'') for regex patterns to avoid escaping issues

tools.name: JSON Handling
examples: with open('data.json') as f: data = json.load(f)
tools.notes: Use json.load() for files and json.loads() for strings

tools.name: HTTP Requests
tools.description: Make HTTP requests using requests library
tools.notes: Install with: pip install requests. Always handle exceptions for network operations

tools.name: Command Line Arguments
tools.description: Parse command line arguments using argparse
tools.notes: Use argparse for complex argument parsing, sys.argv for simple cases

tools.name: Directory Operations
examples: for root, dirs, files in os.walk('.'):
tools.notes: Use pathlib for modern path operations: from pathlib import Path

tools.name: Environment Variables
tools.notes: Use .get() method with default values to avoid KeyError

tools.name: Process Management
examples: import subprocess; result = subprocess.run(['ls', '-la'], capture_output=True, text=True)
tools.notes: Use subprocess.run() for simple cases, Popen for complex process management

tools.name: Date and Time
examples: from datetime import datetime; now = datetime.now()
tools.notes: Use datetime for date operations, time for delays and timestamps

tools.name: Database Operations
tools.notes: Always use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection

tools.name: CSV Processing
examples: import csv; with open('data.csv') as f: reader = csv.DictReader(f)
with open('output.csv', 'w') as f: writer = csv.writer(f)

tools.name: Logging
tools.notes: Use different log levels: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL

tools.name: Error Handling
examples:
try: result = 10 / 0
except ZeroDivisionError: print('Cannot divide by zero')

try: file = open('missing.txt')
except FileNotFoundError as e: print(f'File error: {e}')
tools.notes: Always catch specific exceptions rather than generic Exception when possible

tools.name: List Comprehensions
tools.usage: [expression for item in iterable if condition]
examples: evens = [x for x in range(20) if x % 2 == 0]
tools.notes: More readable and often faster than traditional loops for simple operations

tools.name: Dictionary Operations
tools.notes: Use collections.defaultdict for automatic default values
